caveats: a: Adjacent four-digit occupation proxy rather than a six-digit study.
a: Automation differs by batch size and formulation complexity.
rho: No observed five-year industry adoption measure.
rho: Expert validation remains necessary for material-property and compliance decisions.
e: Injected firm count is margin-bridged and estimated.
e: Classification does not establish independence, EBITDA, or customer diversification.
s5: Economy-wide demographic proxy.
s5: No public control-transfer denominator for 325991.
q: No public contract dataset measures benefit sharing.
q: Proprietary specialties retain more than commodity toll compounding.
d5: Official sources establish process and regulation rather than a direct growth forecast.
d5: Automotive, packaging, construction, and consumer end markets cycle differently.
o: Operator share may migrate to integrated suppliers or customer plants.
o: This estimate is separate from labor reduction within surviving operators.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat B2B toll or product sales of customer-qualified compounds and masterbatches, supported by formulation, testing, color or performance matching, quality documentation, and supply coordination.
lens.excluded_activities: Base-resin polymerization, captive compounding, molding-only operations, pure distribution, shells, and firms outside the EBITDA band.
lens.included_activities: Independent LMM businesses that repeatedly blend, mix, modify, pelletize, or add additives to purchased plastic resins for external customers, including custom formulations and recycled-content compounds.

a.bridge: BLS plastics-manufacturing data show a production-heavy occupation base, including extruding and drawing operators and material-moving workers, but cover NAICS 326 rather than 325991. I translate this adjacent process mix to compounding: formulation search, quality-data interpretation, specifications, scheduling, purchasing, quoting, customer-service triage, and compliance documents are exposed; feeding, blending, extrusion, pelletizing, lab execution, maintenance, warehousing, and material movement are less so. The proxy could overstate exposure if custom compounders are more shop-floor-heavy than the broader comparison.
caveats: Adjacent four-digit occupation proxy rather than a six-digit study.
Automation differs by batch size and formulation complexity.
a.change_evidence: Payroll-weighted task studies at 10-15 compounders, separated by production, laboratory, engineering, quality, sales, planning, and logistics, would materially revise the estimate.
a.rationale: Technical and administrative workflows offer moderate assistance potential, while the core value-creation process remains physical and production-intensive.

caveats: Official sources establish process and regulation rather than a direct growth forecast.
Automotive, packaging, construction, and consumer end markets cycle differently.
d5.change_evidence: Five-year physical-volume forecasts by polymer, end market, additive function, and recycled-content compound, reconciled to customer programs and Census shipments, would materially move the range.
d5.rationale: Plastics remain pervasive intermediate materials, and demand for tailored properties, lightweighting, recycled content, and additive packages supports custom compounding. Manufacturing cycles, resin substitution, downgauging, imports, and pressure to reduce plastic use create downside.

caveats: Injected firm count is margin-bridged and estimated.
Classification does not establish independence, EBITDA, or customer diversification.
e.change_evidence: Classify the injected 91 candidates using business databases, EPA facility records, websites, product capabilities, customer mix, and ownership interviews.
e.rationale: Custom compounding naturally supplies repeat external customers, so most independent LMM operators fit. Captive plants, distributor-heavy firms, molding businesses misclassified into the code, single-customer tollers, weak environmental controls, and obsolete assets reduce eligibility.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 325991 ($1,278,559,146) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 325991 ($14,046,366,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1801 (margins.json[325]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Chemical (Specialty) EBITDA/Sales 18.01%); revenue/employee $657,232 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 325991); class <5: 70 firms, avg revenue/firm $762,500 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $137,326 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 49 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,010,184 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $362,034 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 41 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,894,244 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $701,353 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 91 firms, avg revenue/firm $21,530,637 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,877,668 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 44 firms, avg revenue/firm $94,673,432 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,050,685 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 45 firms, avg revenue/firm $169,109,089 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $30,456,547 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 91

caveats: Operator share may migrate to integrated suppliers or customer plants.
This estimate is separate from labor reduction within surviving operators.
o.change_evidence: Customer make-versus-buy surveys, insourcing announcements, distributor and resin-producer integration trends, and imports of compounded pellets would move the estimate.
o.rationale: Retained demand requires physical mixing, extrusion, pelletizing, testing, and accountable quality release. Software can improve design and operations but cannot supply qualified material; some customers could insource or buy standardized compounds from integrated resin producers.

caveats: No public contract dataset measures benefit sharing.
Proprietary specialties retain more than commodity toll compounding.
q.change_evidence: Customer contracts, formula ownership, price-index clauses, renewal and bid histories, margin-by-SKU data, and customer interviews would directly estimate pass-through.
q.rationale: Customer-qualified formulations, technical collaboration, color and performance consistency, and requalification costs support retention. Tolling, resin-index mechanisms, sophisticated procurement, competitive bids, and transparent input costs pass part of savings to customers.

caveats: No observed five-year industry adoption measure.
Expert validation remains necessary for material-property and compliance decisions.
rho.change_evidence: Plant pilots measuring formulation iterations, lab hours, off-spec rates, schedule performance, quote cycle time, and customer-complaint resolution would move the range.
rho.rationale: Within five years, tools for formulation retrieval, document generation, quality trending, planning, quoting, and customer support are feasible. Proprietary and sparse formulation data, physical trials, legacy controls, product qualification, data integration, and environmental and safety review constrain realized implementation.

s5.bridge: Federal Reserve owner-age chartbooks measure small-business demographics generally rather than compounder transfers. I use age as succession pressure and discount for internal transitions, closures, minority investments, environmental liabilities, and strategic subsidiaries. Consolidation and customer qualification may increase strategic sale interest, while owner dependence can reduce transferability.
caveats: Economy-wide demographic proxy.
No public control-transfer denominator for 325991.
s5.change_evidence: A denominator-based PitchBook/CapIQ/Grata ownership and transaction study of U.S. compounders since 2018, combined with broker and owner interviews, would materially change the range.
s5.rationale: A moderate LMM population and strategic fit with resin distributors, compounders, and plastics processors support deals, but site diligence, cyclicality, customer concentration, and family succession limit qualifying control transfers.

principal_driver: Customer-qualified formulations and repeat physical supply create durable relationships and switching costs.
principal_weakness: Production-heavy operations and transparent resin economics limit labor substitution and retained savings.

sources.supports: Census lists NAICS 325991 Custom Compounding of Purchased Resins, supporting the activity boundary.
sources.title: 2022 North American Industry Classification System Manual

sources.supports: BLS shows a production-oriented adjacent industry and lists 30,760 extruding and drawing machine operators and tenders in the displayed material, used as an occupation proxy.
sources.title: Plastics and Rubber Products Manufacturing: NAICS 326

sources.supports: BLS reports substantial other-production and material-moving employment and identifies chemical equipment and plant operators in adjacent plastics manufacturing, supporting the task-mix proxy.
sources.title: Plastics Product Manufacturing - May 2023 OEWS

sources.supports: EPA states extrusion and pelletizing are used to process plastic materials and describes federal effluent standards, supporting physical-process and compliance claims.
sources.title: Plastics Molding and Forming Effluent Guidelines

sources.supports: Federal Reserve chartbooks provide broad owner-demographic and age-of-owner evidence used only as a succession proxy.
sources.title: 2024 Firms in Focus chartbooks on small business data

sources.supports: EPA describes resin compounding, additive-containing masterbatch, and subsequent extrusion into plastic products, supporting process and continuing material-function claims.
sources.title: EPA Scope Document Describing Resin Compounding and Processing

title: Custom Compounding of Purchased Resins
